source: Incus Server - 6 Profiles: macvlan IP assignment fixes for Rocky Linux and Ubuntu containers

questions.q: Why does macvlan IP assignment misbehave on Rocky Linux 9.4 containers?
options: NetworkManager changes upstream (RHEL) affect macvlan; the assigned interface is not NM-manageable
questions.why: It stems from upstream NetworkManager changes, not Rocky itself; the macvlan interface is not managed by NM.

questions.q: What is the DHCP fix for a Rocky Linux 9 container?
options: Add '@reboot /usr/sbin/dhclient' to root's crontab inside the container
questions.why: A @reboot cron entry runs dhclient on boot so the container picks up its DHCP address.

questions.q: Why does the static-IP fix rename eth0 to net0 in a script?
options: The macvlan interface (eth0) is not manageable by NetworkManager, so it is renamed and configured with ip commands
questions.why: Since the interface is not NM-managed (and network-scripts is deprecated), a script renames it and sets IP/route/DNS, run via @reboot.

questions.q: How does the Ubuntu container differ for macvlan?
options: Ubuntu's NetworkManager does not break macvlan, so DHCP works after assigning the profile
Ubuntu needs dhclient in cron too
questions.why: Ubuntu's implementation works out of the box; a stop/start picks up a DHCP address.

questions.q: How do you set a static IP on the Ubuntu container?
options: Edit its netplan file /etc/netplan/10-incus.yaml (dhcp4 false, addresses, gateway4, nameservers)
questions.why: Ubuntu uses netplan; set dhcp4:false with a static address, gateway and nameservers.
